repository_summary: oss-maintainer-copilot is a Python repository focused on issue triage, PR review preparation, release drafting. A maintainer operations toolkit for GitHub repositories with structured issue triage, PR briefs, release drafts, and repository intelligence.

major_areas.purpose: Core workflow logic, schemas, and CLI code for maintainer automation.
major_areas.contributor_entrypoint: Start here if you want to extend issue triage, PR briefs, release drafting, or repo intelligence.

major_areas.purpose: Fixture-driven regression coverage that keeps outputs stable and reviewable.
major_areas.contributor_entrypoint: Start here to learn expected behavior before changing schemas or workflow heuristics.

major_areas.purpose: Checked-in sample inputs and outputs that show how maintainers will experience the workflows.
major_areas.contributor_entrypoint: Start here if you want the fastest overview of what the project produces today.

major_areas.purpose: Supporting documentation and demo assets for contributors and evaluators.
major_areas.contributor_entrypoint: Start here when you need context, visuals, or project-facing documentation.

major_areas.purpose: GitHub Actions entrypoints for issue, PR, release, and CI automation.
major_areas.contributor_entrypoint: Start here to understand how repository events are wired into maintainer workflows.

good_starting_points: Start with examples/ to see the JSON and markdown outputs expected from each workflow.
Read tests/ next to understand which public outputs are treated as stable.
Move into src/ once you know which workflow you want to extend or debug.
Review .github/workflows/ when you want to understand how repository events trigger automation.
contributor_checklist: Read README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md before changing workflow behavior.
Pick a narrow workflow area or fixture before making broader refactors.
Follow the documented local setup path before editing code or fixtures.
Run pytest after changing schemas, heuristics, or workflow outputs.
Run ruff check . before opening a pull request.
Update examples, fixtures, and docs when public outputs change.
